identity.name: Proficiency Prediction
identity.definition: A Proficiency Prediction is an SPS-generated, model-based estimate of how proficient a specific student is on a specific skill, categorized into an instructional group that tells teachers whether the student needs enrichment, core instruction, or approaching-level support.

representations.heading: Card layout
representations.description: How a Proficiency Prediction appears in skill proficiency grids and dashboards

attributes.name: Proficiency %
attributes.description: Model-estimated proficiency on the linked Skill. 0–100 scale.

attributes.name: Instructional Group
attributes.description: Categorical grouping derived from proficiency: ENRICHMENT, INSTRUCTIONAL, or APPROACHING.

attributes.name: Student ID
attributes.description: The student this prediction applies to.

attributes.name: Student ID

attributes.description: The model's estimate of what percentage of items this student would answer correctly on this skill.

attributes.name: Instructional Group
attributes.description: Categorical grouping derived from proficiency: ENRICHMENT, INSTRUCTIONAL, or APPROACHING.

attributes.name: Prediction Type
attributes.description: Whether the student has Star assessment data (has_star) or only practice data (practice_only).

attributes.name: Prediction Model
attributes.description: Identifier for the SPS model version used to generate this prediction.

attributes.description: When this prediction was generated or last updated.

attributes.name: Skill Is Practiced
attributes.description: Whether the student has practiced this skill in Freckle or other practice tools.

stories.title: View Grid Cell
stories.benefit: I can quickly see at a glance whether each student needs enrichment, core instruction, or approaching-level support for a given skill.
stories.whenClause: I open the SPS proficiency grid for my class,
stories.thenClause: each cell shows the student's proficiency percentage color-coded by instructional group so I can identify intervention needs instantly.

stories.title: Analyze Class Proficiency
stories.benefit: I can plan targeted instruction by identifying which skills have the most students in each instructional group.
stories.whenClause: I view the class-level proficiency summary,
stories.thenClause: predictions are aggregated by skill and instructional group so I can see distribution patterns across my class.

stories.title: Compare Student Skills
stories.benefit: I can identify relative strengths and weaknesses to personalize instruction.
stories.whenClause: I view a single student's skill proficiency profile,
stories.thenClause: all predictions for that student are displayed across skills so I can see which skills need the most attention.

businessRules.title: Uniqueness
businessRules.description: A Proficiency Prediction is uniquely identified by the combination of Student ID + Skill ID. Only one current prediction exists per student-skill pair.

businessRules.title: Generation
businessRules.description: Predictions are system-generated by the SPS model. They cannot be manually created, edited, or deleted by any user role.

businessRules.title: Supersession
businessRules.description: When a new prediction is generated for a student-skill pair, the previous prediction is moved to 'Superseded' state. Only the most recent prediction is shown in the UI by default.

businessRules.title: Instructional Group Assignment
businessRules.description: The instructional group (ENRICHMENT, INSTRUCTIONAL, APPROACHING) is determined by the model based on proficiency thresholds. These thresholds are configurable at the district level.

businessRules.title: Prediction Type
businessRules.description: Predictions with prediction_type 'has_star' incorporate Star assessment data and are considered higher confidence. Predictions with 'practice_only' rely solely on Freckle practice data.

businessRules.title: Skill Practice Flag
businessRules.description: The 'Skill Is Practiced' flag indicates whether the student has engaged with practice items for this skill. Unpracticed skills may have lower confidence predictions.

businessRules.title: Refresh Frequency
businessRules.description: Predictions are regenerated after each Star assessment completion and periodically based on accumulated practice data. The refresh cadence is determined by the SPS model schedule.

businessRules.title: Data Dependencies
businessRules.description: A valid prediction requires at least one data source: a Star assessment score or practice activity data for the student on the linked skill.

businessRules.title: Visibility
businessRules.description: Proficiency Predictions are visible to Teachers and Admins only. Students and parents do not see raw prediction data. Predictions are surfaced through the SPS proficiency grid UI.

businessRules.title: Aggregation
businessRules.description: Predictions can be aggregated at the class, school, or district level to show instructional group distributions across students for a given skill or standard.

businessRules.title: Standard Mapping
businessRules.description: Skills referenced by predictions are mapped to state standards. Predictions can be rolled up to the standard level for standards-based reporting.

states.name: Current
states.description: The most recent prediction for this student-skill pair, actively displayed in the proficiency grid
states.triggers: SPS model generates or refreshes a prediction

states.name: Superseded
states.description: A previous prediction replaced by a newer one; retained for historical analysis
states.triggers: A new prediction is generated for the same student-skill pair

relationships.mechanics: Each prediction is linked to exactly one Student. The prediction is system-generated based on the student's assessment and practice data.
relationships.cardinality: Many-to-one. A Student has many Proficiency Predictions (one per skill); each Prediction belongs to exactly one Student.
relationships.sorts: Predictions for a student: by proficiency % (ascending/descending), by skill name alphabetically, by instructional group.
relationships.filters: By instructional group (ENRICHMENT/INSTRUCTIONAL/APPROACHING), by prediction type (has_star/practice_only), by practiced status.
relationships.dependencies: If a Student is deactivated, their predictions remain for historical analysis but are excluded from active class-level aggregations.

relationships.mechanics: Each prediction is linked to exactly one Skill. The prediction estimates the student's proficiency on that specific skill.
relationships.cardinality: Many-to-one. A Skill has many Proficiency Predictions (one per student); each Prediction targets exactly one Skill.
relationships.sorts: Predictions for a skill: by student name alphabetically, by proficiency % (ascending/descending), by instructional group.
relationships.filters: By instructional group, by prediction type, by student demographics (via Student relationship).
relationships.dependencies: If a Skill is deprecated or remapped, associated predictions may be superseded or recalculated against the replacement skill.

allAttributes.name: Student ID
allAttributes.description: The student this prediction applies to.

allAttributes.name: Proficiency %
allAttributes.description: Model-estimated proficiency on the linked Skill. 0–100 scale.

allAttributes.name: Predicted % Correct
allAttributes.description: The model's estimate of what percentage of items this student would answer correctly on this skill.

allAttributes.name: Instructional Group
allAttributes.description: Categorical grouping: ENRICHMENT (above grade-level proficiency), INSTRUCTIONAL (at grade-level, core instruction), or APPROACHING (below grade-level, needs support).

allAttributes.name: Prediction Type
allAttributes.description: Data source indicator: has_star (Star assessment + practice data) or practice_only (Freckle practice data only).

allAttributes.name: Prediction Model
allAttributes.description: Identifier for the SPS model version used to generate this prediction (e.g., 'SPS v3.2').

allAttributes.name: Prediction Timestamp
allAttributes.description: When this prediction was generated or last refreshed by the SPS model.

allAttributes.name: Skill Is Practiced
allAttributes.description: Whether the student has engaged with practice items for this skill in Freckle or other practice tools.

structure.evidence: A Proficiency Prediction has clear attributes: proficiency percentage, predicted percent correct, instructional group, prediction type, model version, timestamp, and practiced status. It can be displayed in grid cells, rows, and detail panels.

instances.evidence: Harry Potter — 82% proficiency on 'Determine Meaning of Words' (ENRICHMENT). Hermione Granger — 54% on 'Analyze Text Structure' (INSTRUCTIONAL). Ron Weasley — 31% on 'Use Context Clues' (APPROACHING). A class of 25 students across 30 skills produces 750 prediction instances.

purpose.evidence: Teachers use Proficiency Predictions to identify which students need enrichment, core instruction, or intervention for each skill. Predictions drive the SPS proficiency grid, inform instructional grouping decisions, and connect Star assessment data to actionable classroom guidance.

sipValidation.verdict: Core data/AI object. The bridge between Star assessments and actionable instructional grouping for teachers.

synonyms.notes: Short form used in code and internal references
Sometimes used interchangeably when referring to the proficiency percentage
More precise term used in model documentation and research papers

shapeshifterMatrix.description: A single cell in the skills proficiency grid, color-coded by instructional group. Teachers scan the grid to spot patterns across students and skills.
Prediction details shown on hover — proficiency percentage, predicted accuracy, and whether the skill has been practiced. Provides context without leaving the grid.
Aggregated proficiency predictions rolled up to the standard level. Teachers see how students are predicted to perform across a full standard, not just individual skills.
A student's proficiency predictions listed as data rows with prediction type and practice status. Teachers review predicted performance across skills for an individual student.
